heading: Question S1Q1
text:
Which of the following are
matter?
Chair, air, love, smell, hate,
almonds, thought, cold, lemon
water, smell of perfume.

heading: Question S1Q2
text:
Give reasons for the following
observation:
The smell of hot sizzling food
reaches you several metres
away, but to get the smell from
cold food you have to go close.

heading: Question S1Q3
text:
A diver is able to cut through
water in a swimming pool. Which
property of matter does this
observation show?

heading: Question S1Q4
text:
What are the characteristics of
the particles of matter?

heading: Question S2Q1
text:
The mass per unit volume of a
substance is called density.
(density = mass/volume).
Arrange the following in order of
increasing density - air, exhaust
from chimneys, honey, water,
chalk, cotton and iron.

heading: Question S2Q4
text:
Liquids generally have lower
density as compared to solids.
But you must have observed that
ice floats on water. Find out why.

heading: Question S3Q2
text:
What is the physical state of
water at:
a. 250oC b. 100oC ?

heading: Question S3Q3
text:
For any substance, why does the
temperature remain constant
during the change of state?

heading: Question S3Q4
text:
Suggest a method to liquefy
atmospheric gases.

heading: Question S4Q1
text:
Why does a desert cooler cool
better on a hot dry day?

heading: Question S4Q2
text:
How does the water kept in an
earthen pot (matka) become cool
during summer?

heading: Question S4Q3
text:
Why does our palm feel cold
when we put some acetone or
petrol or perfume on it?

heading: Question S4Q4
text:
Why are we able to sip hot tea or
milk faster from a saucer rather
than a cup?

heading: Question S4Q5
text:
What type of clothes should we
wear in summer?
